title: Allophylus distachys
intro: Allophylus distachys, also known as African almond, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Sapindaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Allophylus distachys include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Allophylus distachys is often used to prepare traditional herbal remedies, such as infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions and to promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Allophylus distachys belongs to the family Sapindaceae. This family consists of over 130 genera of flowering plants. Allophylus distachys is a species of flowering plant within the genus Allophylus, which is native to tropical Africa. The Sapindaceae family is part of the larger order Sapindales, which is a group of plants that also includes the mimosoid legumes. Allophylus distachys is a shrub or small tree that is often found in woodland and forest environments.
